outcome_summary: PDG papers extraction Session γ — foundational reading. Authored engine/build_readiness/pdg_papers_extraction/foundations.md (473 lines) answering F1-F4 from kant_walkthrough.md §6.6 via WebSearch + WebFetch + model knowledge with explicit four-tier evidence markers ([primary-verified] / [secondary-source] / [model-knowledge] / [hedged]) on every load-bearing claim. Key findings: F1 — threshold-concept framework cleanly distinguishes phenomenology-as-method (threshold-shaped) from phenomenology-as-movement (not threshold-shaped), partially explaining the kant_walkthrough.md §3.2.1.A multi-typing tension via movement/method conflation in the node-as-authored. F2 NEGATIVE RESULT — `bridge_concept` is a synthesis-paper coinage NOT literature-grounded in Spiro's cognitive flexibility theory; closest lit-grounded alternative is `boundary_concept` per Star & Griesemer 1989 / Akkerman & Bakker 2011. F3 — every threshold concept is a bottleneck (subset relationship); cognitive-vs-emotional bottleneck bipartition well-attested; proposed Cluster 4 enum lacks `bottleneck`; four readings provided for whether/how to admit bottleneck-related enum values. F4 — majority reading (Zahavi et al. via SEP) supports kant_walkthrough.md §5.2.1 framing of phenomenology-vs-introspection; minority refining reading (Tewes 2018 Frontiers) argues phenomenology IS refined introspection. Recommended nuanced remediation_note text drafted naming the naive-vs-refined distinction. Plus bonus §F5 on Falmagne knowledge spaces (forward pointer for Session δ-or-later canonical-source authoring). §7.2 per-D-item bearing table maps F1-F4 onto kant_walkthrough.md §6.7 D1-D8: F1 + F2 + F3 + F4 all bear on D2 (single-valued vs multi-valued node_type); F2 + F3 bear on D8 (open-for-revision enums); F4 bears on D6 (unlearning_required_before endpoint) + D7 (seed-orphaned misconception handling). §7.3 two Session δ recommendations: (1) adopt multi-valued node_type array form; (2) treat node_type enum as open-for-revision per D8 with first revision authored alongside the migration. Quality-first posture holds — no Issues fire, no ADRs land, no decisions settled — Session δ adjudicates. Inline housekeeping per user direction at boot: released the 167MB S-0193 mempalace backup tarball (both retention gates met — 5 post-cutover sessions confirmed clean engine_memory operation per S-0194 verdict-PASS + S-0195 + S-0196 + S-0199 + S-0200, and user OK-to-delete signaled); pruned the resolved 'S-0193 mempalace backup retention + residual prose disposition' section from HANDOFF.md per the S-0121-pattern prune-on-resolve discipline; removed the 'Backup retention' bullet from engine/STATE.md 'Carried open items' block. Saved engine_memory lesson drawer (id c90409ebdd48...) generalizing the F2 + F3 pattern: per-enum-value literature checks find real defects in synthesis-paper schema proposals (~10 minutes per enum value via WebSearch yields citation-able findings for ADR Decision sections). Three commits this session — eager-claim 11a50d8, foundations.md 13f2135, housekeeping 716f066 — plus this close commit. The cadence trigger does NOT fire this session per ADR 0022 (slots_since = S-0201 - S-0184 = 17 < cadence 20; due at S-0204).
